title: The Butterfly's Numidian Gown by Emily Dickinson — Deep Poetry Lines
content:
The Butterfly's Numidian Gown
With spots of Burnish roasted on
Is proof against the Sun
Yet prone to shut its spotted Fan
And panting on a Clover lean
As if it were undone --
meaning: "The Butterfly's Numidian Gown" by Emily Dickinson is a deep poem consisting of 6 lines. The poet explores themes of deep through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "The Butterfly's Numidian Gown With spots of Burnish roasted on...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Emily Dickinson's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
